text.title: Python Async Concurrency Primitives
text.summary: TaskGroup, gather, Semaphore, timeouts, and async generators — when and how to use each.

rule.statement: Use asyncio.TaskGroup for any set of concurrent coroutines; prefer it over gather for new code on Python 3.11+.
rule.rationale: TaskGroup propagates cancellation automatically — if one task raises, all siblings are cancelled, preventing zombie tasks that gather leaves running.

example.code: async def process_all(items: list[str]) -> list[str]: results = [] async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as tg: tasks = [tg.create_task(process_item(item)) for item in items] # All tasks completed here; any exception cancels siblings return [t.result() for t in tasks]

rule.statement: Wrap every external-call fan-out with asyncio.Semaphore; derive the limit from downstream capacity, not an arbitrary number.
rule.rationale: Unbounded gather over a queue can spawn 100k tasks, consuming all RAM and crushing downstream services.

example.code:
async def fetch_all(urls: list[str], max_concurrent: int = 10) -> list[dict]: semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrent)
async def fetch_one(url: str) -> dict | None: async with semaphore: async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: r = await client.get(url, timeout=30.0) r.raise_for_status() return r.json()
results = await asyncio.gather(*[fetch_one(u) for u in urls], return_exceptions=True) return [r for r in results if not isinstance(r, Exception)]

rule.statement: Pair every await with a timeout using asyncio.timeout() (3.11+) or asyncio.wait_for(); never await without a deadline.
rule.rationale: Awaiting without a timeout means a stalled downstream silently hangs the event loop task indefinitely.

example.code: async def with_timeout(): try: async with asyncio.timeout(2.0): result = await slow_operation() return result except asyncio.TimeoutError: return None

rule.statement: Use async generators for paginated data streams to avoid loading all pages into memory.
rule.rationale: Loading all pages first wastes memory and delays first-item processing; generators yield items as they arrive.

example.code:
from typing import AsyncIterator
async def fetch_pages(base_url: str) -> AsyncIterator[dict]: page = 1 async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: while True: r = await client.get(f"{base_url}?page={page}") data = r.json() if not data["items"]: break for item in data["items"]: yield item page += 1
async def process_stream(): async for item in fetch_pages("https://api.example.com/items"): await handle(item)
